_note: Stub data for development. Replace with live API calls in production.

politicians.name: Donald Trump
politicians.bio: 45th and 47th President of the United States. Former real estate developer and television personality, known for America First trade policy, immigration restrictions, and broad use of executive authority.

bills.title: One Big Beautiful Bill Act
bills.summary: Omnibus reconciliation bill extending the 2017 tax cuts, funding border wall construction, and rolling back clean energy incentives.

controversies.title: January 6 Capitol Attack
controversies.description: Trump was impeached for a second time after his supporters stormed the U.S. Capitol on January 6, 2021. He was acquitted by the Senate. A subsequent federal indictment was dropped after his 2024 election victory.

politicians.name: JD Vance
politicians.bio: 50th Vice President of the United States. Former Senator from Ohio and author of Hillbilly Elegy. Shifted from a Trump critic in 2016 to one of his most loyal allies by 2022.

bills.title: Border Security and Immigration Enforcement Act
bills.summary: Legislation to fund additional border barriers, expand deportation authority, and reinstate Remain in Mexico policy.

controversies.title: 'Childless cat ladies' comment
controversies.description: Vance referred to Democratic leaders as 'childless cat ladies who are miserable' in a 2021 Tucker Carlson interview, drawing widespread condemnation.

controversies.title: Private anti-Trump messages from 2016
controversies.description: Text messages leaked in 2022 revealed Vance had privately called Trump 'America's Hitler' and 'cultural heroin' in 2016, before becoming one of Trump's staunchest Senate allies.

politicians.name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
politicians.bio: Representative for New York's 14th congressional district. Co-author of the Green New Deal and the most-followed member of Congress on social media.

bills.title: Green New Deal Resolution
bills.summary: Congressional resolution outlining goals for addressing climate change and economic inequality through a federal jobs guarantee and clean energy transition.

controversies.title: 'Tax the Rich' Met Gala dress
controversies.description: AOC wore a gown with 'Tax the Rich' printed on the back to the 2021 Met Gala, a $35,000-per-ticket fundraiser. Critics called the gesture hypocritical.

politicians.name: Bernie Sanders
politicians.bio: Senior Senator from Vermont and longest-serving independent in congressional history. Two-time Democratic presidential candidate known for Medicare for All, free college tuition, and wealth tax proposals.

bills.title: Medicare for All Act of 2023
bills.summary: Establishes a national single-payer health insurance program replacing private insurance, covering all Americans including prescription drugs, dental, and vision.

bills.title: College for All Act
bills.summary: Eliminates tuition and fees at four-year public colleges and universities and cancels all student loan debt.

controversies.title: Three houses controversy
controversies.description: Sanders, a frequent critic of the wealthy, faced criticism when it emerged he owns three homes, including a $575,000 lakefront vacation property in Vermont, seen by some as inconsistent with his income inequality message.

politicians.name: Ted Cruz
politicians.bio: Senior Senator from Texas. Former Solicitor General of Texas and two-time presidential candidate. Prominent conservative voice on constitutional law, energy policy, and border security.

bills.title: Secure the Border Act
bills.summary: Mandates completion of a physical border barrier, reinstates Remain in Mexico, and ends catch-and-release immigration enforcement policies.

bills.title: TECH Act — Targeting Enforcement of Chinese Hegemony
bills.summary: Prohibits federal contracts with companies that transfer sensitive technology to China and expands sanctions authority over Chinese military-linked entities.

controversies.title: Cancun trip during Texas winter storm
controversies.description: Cruz flew to Cancun, Mexico with his family during the February 2021 Texas winter storm that killed over 240 people and left millions without power. He initially blamed the trip on his daughters before returning early amid public outrage.

controversies.title: 2020 election objection
controversies.description: Cruz led a group of Senate Republicans in objecting to the certification of the 2020 presidential election results in Pennsylvania and Arizona, even after the January 6 Capitol attack.

politicians.name: Nancy Pelosi
politicians.bio: Representative for California's 11th congressional district and former Speaker of the House (2007-2011, 2019-2023). First woman to serve as Speaker. Architect of the Affordable Care Act and led two impeachments of Donald Trump.

bills.title: STOCK Act (co-sponsor)
bills.summary: Prohibits members of Congress and federal employees from using non-public information for stock trading and requires disclosure of securities transactions.

controversies.title: Congressional stock trading scrutiny
controversies.description: Pelosi and her husband Paul Pelosi's stock trades, particularly in tech companies subject to legislation Pelosi oversaw, drew repeated calls for congressional stock trading bans. She initially opposed such bans before reversing her position in 2021.

controversies.title: Tearing up Trump's State of the Union speech
controversies.description: Pelosi tore up a copy of Trump's 2020 State of the Union address on live television immediately after he finished speaking, a gesture widely seen as unprecedented for a sitting Speaker.

politicians.name: Mitch McConnell
politicians.bio: Senior Senator from Kentucky and longest-serving Senate party leader in U.S. history. Former Senate Majority Leader (2015-2021, 2025) and Minority Leader. Known for blocking Merrick Garland's Supreme Court nomination and confirming three Trump justices.

bills.title: Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (co-negotiator)
bills.summary: Invests $1.2 trillion in roads, bridges, broadband, and water systems over five years. McConnell was among 19 Senate Republicans who voted for the bill.

controversies.title: Merrick Garland Supreme Court blockade
controversies.description: McConnell refused to hold hearings for President Obama's Supreme Court nominee Merrick Garland for nearly a year in 2016, citing the presidential election year, then reversed that standard in 2020 to confirm Amy Coney Barrett weeks before the election.

controversies.title: Neurological episodes during press conferences
controversies.description: McConnell froze mid-sentence at two separate press conferences in 2023, sparking public and congressional debate about his health and fitness for office. His office stated the episodes were a result of lightheadedness.

politicians.name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
politicians.bio: Representative for Georgia's 14th congressional district. Prominent figure in the House Freedom Caucus and close Trump ally. Filed multiple motions to vacate against Speaker Mike Johnson.

bills.title: Protect Children's Innocence Act
bills.summary: Criminalizes gender-affirming medical care for minors, including hormone therapy and surgical procedures, making it a federal felony.

controversies.title: Promotion of QAnon and election conspiracy theories
controversies.description: Before her 2020 election, Greene promoted QAnon conspiracies and suggested the Parkland and Sandy Hook school shootings were staged. She was stripped of her committee assignments in February 2021 by a House vote.

controversies.title: Motion to vacate against Speaker Mike Johnson
controversies.description: Greene filed multiple motions to vacate the chair against Speaker Mike Johnson in 2024, citing his support for Ukraine aid. The motions were tabled with bipartisan support.

politicians.name: Rand Paul
politicians.bio: Junior Senator from Kentucky and physician. Son of former Representative Ron Paul. A libertarian-leaning Republican known for opposing foreign aid, surveillance programs, and federal spending.

bills.title: FISA Reform and Reauthorization Act opposition
bills.summary: Paul led Senate opposition to FISA Section 702 reauthorization, arguing it enabled warrantless surveillance of American citizens without sufficient judicial oversight.

bills.title: Audit the Fed Act
bills.summary: Requires a full and complete audit of the Federal Reserve's Board of Governors and Federal Open Market Committee, including monetary policy decisions.

controversies.title: Neighbor assault
controversies.description: In 2017, Paul's neighbor Rene Boucher tackled him from behind while he was mowing his lawn, breaking six of his ribs. Boucher pleaded guilty to assault and was sentenced to 30 days in prison.

controversies.title: COVID-19 Senate vote while awaiting test results
controversies.description: Paul voted in the Senate and used the Senate gym while awaiting the results of a COVID-19 test in March 2020. He later tested positive, drawing criticism from colleagues.

politicians.name: Elizabeth Warren
politicians.bio: Senior Senator from Massachusetts and former Harvard Law professor. 2020 presidential candidate. A leading progressive voice on financial regulation, consumer protection, and corporate accountability.

bills.title: Ultra-Millionaire Tax Act
bills.summary: Imposes a 2% annual wealth tax on net worth above $50 million and a 3% tax on net worth above $1 billion to fund universal child care, student loan cancellation, and other programs.

bills.title: American Anti-Corruption Act (draft framework)
bills.summary: Proposes lifetime lobbying bans for senior officials, public campaign financing, and mandatory disclosure of all political donations.

controversies.title: Native American ancestry claim
controversies.description: Warren claimed Native American ancestry for years and listed herself as a minority in a legal directory. A 2018 DNA test showed distant Native ancestry (6-10 generations back). The Cherokee Nation and many Indigenous groups criticized her use of the claim.

politicians.name: Matt Gaetz
politicians.bio: Former Representative for Florida's 1st congressional district (2017-2024). Resigned from Congress in November 2024 after being nominated by Trump for Attorney General, a nomination he later withdrew amid a House Ethics Committee investigation.

bills.title: Motion to Vacate — Speaker Kevin McCarthy
bills.summary: Gaetz filed and passed the first successful motion to vacate the chair in U.S. history, removing Kevin McCarthy as Speaker of the House in October 2023.

controversies.title: House Ethics Committee sex trafficking investigation
controversies.description: The House Ethics Committee investigated Gaetz for alleged sex trafficking, illicit drug use, and providing false information to investigators. The committee was poised to release its report when Gaetz resigned from Congress in November 2024.

controversies.title: Ouster of Speaker McCarthy
controversies.description: Gaetz orchestrated the historic removal of Speaker Kevin McCarthy in October 2023, making McCarthy the first Speaker ever removed by a motion to vacate. The move was widely criticized within the Republican Party for causing weeks of House paralysis.

politicians.name: Ro Khanna
politicians.bio: Representative for California's 17th congressional district (Silicon Valley). Known for tech policy, economic populism, and a non-interventionist foreign policy that sometimes puts him at odds with Democratic leadership.

bills.title: No First Use Act
bills.summary: Prohibits the United States from using nuclear weapons first in any armed conflict, requiring a formal declaration of war from Congress before any nuclear strike.

bills.title: Worker Dividend Act
bills.summary: Requires companies with more than $100M in annual revenue to distribute a percentage of stock buybacks directly to non-executive employees.

controversies.title: Initial opposition to Ukraine aid supplemental
controversies.description: Khanna was among a small group of progressive House Democrats who initially opposed Ukraine aid supplementals citing deficit concerns and the need for a diplomatic framework, drawing criticism from party leadership.

politicians.name: Elon Musk
politicians.bio: Billionaire entrepreneur and head of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, owner of X (formerly Twitter). Oversaw sweeping cuts to the federal workforce.

controversies.title: DOGE mass federal workforce reductions
controversies.description: As DOGE head, Musk oversaw the termination of tens of thousands of federal workers and the near-dismantling of USAID. Multiple federal courts issued injunctions blocking parts of the effort.
